summary: Iteration 3 testing completed with 100% backend tests passed (6/6) and all frontend player features verified. New /api/stream/url endpoint works correctly for live and movie streams. Player screen at /player route fully functional with navigation from Home, Live TV, and VOD tabs. All player UI components present: back button, play/pause controls, LIVE badge, EPG info panel, stream type display. All 5 tabs render correctly after player addition. CRITICAL BUG FOUND & FIXED: Live TV navigation failed due to missing router hook - fixed by adding const router = useRouter(); to live.tsx line 16.

integration_issues.flow: Live TV channel navigation to player
integration_issues.issue: Clicking live channel threw 'router is not defined' error at live.tsx:110
integration_issues.root_cause: Missing const router = useRouter(); hook declaration in LiveScreen component
integration_issues.fix_applied: Added const router = useRouter(); at line 16 in live.tsx

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all navigation flows and player features working after fix)

seed_data_creation: No seed data created. Testing performed with valid Xtream Codes credentials (DJBIGANT).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration 3 features tested and working. Backend /api/stream/url endpoint returns url, fallback_url, raw_url for both live (m3u8) and movie (container extension) streams. Player screen at /player route with expo-video integration fully functional. Navigation works from Home hero button, Live TV channels, and VOD movies. Player UI has all required components: back button, play/pause, LIVE badge for live streams, EPG info panel with program details, stream type display. All 5 tabs (Home, Live, VOD, Series, Catch-Up) render correctly. Bug in Live TV navigation was found and fixed (missing router hook). expo-video VideoView component may not render in web preview but native controls work correctly.

bug_rca.bug_description: Live TV channel click threw 'Uncaught Error: router is not defined' at app/(tabs)/live.tsx line 110
bug_rca.root_cause: live.tsx imported useRouter from expo-router at line 7, but never called the hook to create router instance. The playChannel function at line 109 tried to use router.push() without router being defined.
bug_rca.fix_applied: Added const router = useRouter(); at line 16 in LiveScreen component, after other hook calls (useTheme, useAuth)
bug_rca.verification: After fix and Expo restart, navigation from Live TV → player works correctly. No more 'router is not defined' errors.

code_review_completed: Backend /api/stream/url endpoint properly handles live and movie stream types
Backend resolves LB redirects with HEAD request and follow_redirects=False
Backend returns correct m3u8 URL for live streams, mp4/mkv for movies
Player screen uses useVideoPlayer and VideoView from expo-video
Player screen properly passes streamId, streamName, streamType params via router
Player screen resolves stream URL via api.getStreamUrl()
Player screen loads EPG data for live streams
Player screen saves to watch history
All navigation flows (live.tsx, vod.tsx, home.tsx) use router.push() correctly
All player UI components have proper testIDs for testing
Player screen uses SafeAreaView for proper mobile spacing

rca_of_issue.issue: Live TV navigation to player failed with 'router is not defined' error
rca_of_issue.error_message: Uncaught Error: router is not defined at app/(tabs)/live.tsx:110:5
reproduction_steps: 4. Error thrown: router is not defined
rca_of_issue.root_cause_analysis: The live.tsx file imported useRouter from expo-router (line 7) but never called the hook to instantiate the router object. The component had useTheme() and useAuth() hooks but was missing const router = useRouter(). When playChannel() function tried to call router.push() at line 110, router was undefined.
rca_of_issue.fix_implementation: Added const router = useRouter(); at line 16 in LiveScreen component, immediately after useAuth() hook and before state declarations.
rca_of_issue.testing_verification: After fix: (1) Restarted Expo to clear Metro cache, (2) Navigated to Live TV tab, (3) Clicked live channel, (4) Successfully navigated to player screen, (5) Verified all player components present, (6) Verified back button returns to Live TV. No more errors.
rca_of_issue.prevention: Always ensure router hooks are called when using Expo Router navigation. Add ESLint rule to check for router usage without hook declaration.
